identity.name: The Spice Trader & The Olive Pit

narrative.summary: Spice apothecary + olive oil tasting bar under one roof. Walls of jars, experiential shopping. Design-forward space. 4.8★ (205 reviews).

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on Queen West, walking into what looks more like a spice apothecary than a shop—floor-to-ceiling walls lined with glass jars, each with a sample you can uncap and smell like you're testing perfume. The design is clean and minimalist, the kind of place where the product does the talking, and one side of the room transitions into an olive oil tasting bar where you can actually try before committing. It smells like toasted cumin and dried lime and something floral you can't quite place.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: This isn't about prepared food—it's about the ingredients you take home. The whole spices rotate seasonally and come in screw-top containers that actually stay fresh, from Urfa biber and Aleppo pepper to bay leaves so fragrant you'll understand why everyone raves about them. On the olive oil side, you're tasting single-origin pressings and aged balsamics, the kind that rewrite what you thought grocery store bottles could do. Spices run fair for the quality—a jar will set you back around $8-15 depending on rarity—and the olive oils start around $20.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: Go on a weekend afternoon when you have time to smell everything, uncap every sample jar, and talk to the staff about what actually makes Tellicherry peppercorns different. Or stop by solo mid-week when you're replacing that stale cinnamon you've been ignoring in your cupboard and need someone to tell you which one to buy.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Easily my favourite spice shop in the city. The spices are fresh and fragrant with stock rotating regularly. You can immediately taste the difference, and it is not subtle – even the bay leaves are shockingly good. Respectable selection of dried chilis and the pricing is fair for the quality offered. I am pleased they have finally settled on the screw top container, the objectively better design (I will die on this hill). Highly recommended!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "the highest quality spices you can find in the city. they have a sample jar for each of their spices, just like a candle store, to make sure you can get a good whiff of each spice before purchasing.

slowly need to replace all my bland, previously bought spices with The Spice Trader spices."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Smashing IT!

This store has everything that you need to enhance your culinary talents and tickle your taste buds.

Great customer service and amazing range of delectable STUFF to make that special dish that much special.

If you're a bread dunker like me, then you're going to want to pick up one of their amazing wide selections of balsamic vinegars.

From spices to finishing salts, the variety and choices are many.

The staff are also extremely knowledgeable, which is an advantage if you're not sure which item to pick from regarding what it is that you're preparing etc.

The Spice Trader & The Olive Pit is unique and rare gem in the city and you owe it to your taste buds to give it a visit.

Check them out, you'll be very happy that you did."
